basic_details.job_title: DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice Recruitment 2026: 20 Vacancies Open for Library Science, Computer Science & Photography
basic_details.short_information: Defence Scientific Information & Documentation Centre (DESIDOC), a unit of DRDO, has invited applications for apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act, 1961 for the financial year 2026–2027. A total of 20 vacancies (subject to increase or decrease) are available across three disciplines - Library & Information Science (16 seats), Computer Science (2 seats), and Photography/Videography (2 seats). Applications must be submitted within 21 days from the date the advertisement appears on the DRDO website.

application_fee.details: The notification does not mention any application fee. No fee details have been stated for any category.

age_limit.details: The upper age limit is 28 years as on the date of written test/interview. Relaxation of five years is available for SC/ST candidates and three years for OBC candidates.

qualification.details: Only candidates who completed their qualifying course in 2024 or 2025 are eligible. Candidates who passed before 2024 are not eligible. Candidates holding post-graduate qualifications are also not eligible to apply. | Candidates who have already undergone training or have job experience of one year or more after completing their qualifying qualification are not eligible for engagement as apprentices under the Act. | The upper age limit is 28 years as on the date of written test/interview. Relaxation of five years is available for SC/ST candidates and three years for OBC candidates. | Candidates who have previously been terminated from apprenticeship training at any organisation or institute are not eligible to apply. | Before applying, every candidate must register on http://www.mhrdnats.gov.in or enrol on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) portal at http://nats.education.gov.in. Applications from unregistered candidates are liable to be rejected.

salary_details.details: Apprentices will receive a monthly stipend - ₹12,300 per month for graduates and B.E. holders, and ₹10,900 per month for diploma and ITI holders. This is not a salary; completion of training does not confer any right to employment or appointment in DESIDOC or DRDO.

selection_process: Candidates will first be shortlisted on merit (percentage/marks in the essential qualification). Shortlisted candidates will then appear for a written test and/or interview. The selection mode may be modified based on organisational requirements. A Selection Board constituted by the Director
DESIDOC will scrutinise all applications and select candidates. Only selected candidates will be informed via offer letter or email.
Shortlisted candidates must bring all original certificates at the time of document verification/interview. No TA/DA will be paid for attending any stage of the selection process.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted physically - either by post or in person. All correspondence thereafter will be conducted exclusively by email, so providing a correct email address is critical.
Register on the NATS portal at http://www.mhrdnats.gov.in or http://nats.education.gov.in and note your enrolment number.
Download the application form from the DRDO website at www.drdo.gov.in (look in the "What's New" section).
Fill in the form completely and attach self-attested photocopies of all required documents.
SC/ST/OBC candidates must attach a valid caste certificate in the prescribed Central Government format.
Superscribe the envelope with the name of the discipline applied for.
Submit by post or by hand to: Director, Defence Scientific Information & Documentation Centre (DESIDOC), Metcalfe House, Civil Lines, Delhi – 110 054, within 21 days of the advertisement date.

Downloading and printing the application form is the candidate's own responsibility. DESIDOC will not be responsible for any loss of email or application due to an incorrect email ID, spam filters, or other technical reasons.

content_sections.heading: DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Quick summary

rows.desidoc_drdo_delhi: Library & Information Science · Computer Science · Photography/Videography
Within 21 days from date of advertisement on www.drdo.gov.in
By post or by hand to DESIDOC, Delhi

content_sections.heading: DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy breakdown
content_sections.text: There are three disciplines open for apprenticeship engagement. Library & Information Science carries the largest share with 16 seats, while Computer Science and Photography/Videography each have 2 seats. The organisation reserves the right to revise these numbers without notice. * Numbers may increase or decrease per organisational requirements.

content_sections.heading: DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility criteria
content_sections.text: Only candidates who completed their qualifying course in 2024 or 2025 are eligible. Candidates who passed before 2024 are not eligible. Candidates holding post-graduate qualifications are also not eligible to apply. Candidates who have already undergone training or have job experience of one year or more after completing their qualifying qualification are not eligible for engagement as apprentices under the Act. The upper age limit is 28 years as on the date of written test/interview. Relaxation of five years is available for SC/ST candidates and three years for OBC candidates. Candidates who have previously been terminated from apprenticeship training at any organisation or institute are not eligible to apply. Before applying, every candidate must register on http://www.mhrdnats.gov.in or enrol on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) portal at http://nats.education.gov.in . Applications from unregistered candidates are liable to be rejected.

rows.required_qualification: Graduation + Degree in Library & Information Science (one year) from a recognised university/institute
Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science or Three-year Diploma in Computer Science from a recognised university/institute
Three-year Diploma in Photography or Videography or ITI Certificate from a recognised university/institute

content_sections.heading: Application fee
content_sections.text: The notification does not mention any application fee. No fee details have been stated for any category.

content_sections.heading: How to apply
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted physically - either by post or in person. All correspondence thereafter will be conducted exclusively by email, so providing a correct email address is critical. Downloading and printing the application form is the candidate's own responsibility. DESIDOC will not be responsible for any loss of email or application due to an incorrect email ID, spam filters, or other technical reasons.

content_sections.heading: Stipend / pay
content_sections.text: Apprentices will receive a monthly stipend - ₹12,300 per month for graduates and B.E. holders, and ₹10,900 per month for diploma and ITI holders. This is not a salary; completion of training does not confer any right to employment or appointment in DESIDOC or DRDO.

content_sections.heading: Selection process
content_sections.text: Candidates will first be shortlisted on merit (percentage/marks in the essential qualification). Shortlisted candidates will then appear for a written test and/or interview . The selection mode may be modified based on organisational requirements. A Selection Board constituted by the Director, DESIDOC will scrutinise all applications and select candidates. Only selected candidates will be informed via offer letter or email. Shortlisted candidates must bring all original certificates at the time of document verification/interview. No TA/DA will be paid for attending any stage of the selection process.

content_sections.heading: Terms and conditions
content_sections.text: Selected candidates must execute a contract of apprenticeship for 12 months under the Apprentices Act, 1961. A medical fitness certificate must be submitted at the time of joining. No quarters, hostel accommodation, or transport will be provided by DESIDOC during the training period. Suppression of facts or submission of false information will lead to disqualification at any stage. Any attempt to influence the selection process will result in immediate cancellation of candidature.

content_sections.heading: Troubleshooting & common mistakes
content_sections.text: Applying for government apprenticeships involves a few common failure points that are worth checking before you hit submit.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for DRDO DESIDOC Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted within 21 days from the date the advertisement is published on the DRDO website (www.drdo.gov.in). Check the "What's New" section of the website for the exact publication date.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available?
faq.answer: A total of 20 seats are notified — 16 in Library & Information Science, 2 in Computer Science, and 2 in Photography/Videography. The number may increase or decrease based on organisational requirements.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for DESIDOC apprenticeship 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 28 years as on the date of the written test/interview. SC/ST candidates get a relaxation of 5 years and OBC candidates get 3 years.

faq.question: Q4. Which pass-out years are eligible?
faq.answer: Only candidates who completed their qualifying course in 2024 or 2025 are eligible. Candidates who passed before 2024 and those holding post-graduate qualifications are not eligible.

faq.question: Q5. What is the stipend for DESIDOC apprentices?
faq.answer: Graduate and B.E. holders receive ₹12,300 per month. Diploma and ITI holders receive ₹10,900 per month.

faq.question: Q6. How do I apply for DESIDOC apprenticeship 2026?
faq.answer: First register on the NATS portal (mhrdnats.gov.in or nats.education.gov.in), then download the application form from www.drdo.gov.in, fill it with self-attested document copies, and send it by post or hand-deliver it to DESIDOC, Metcalfe House, Civil Lines, Delhi – 110 054.

faq.question: Q7. Is there a prior experience restriction?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates who have already completed one year or more of training or job experience after their qualifying qualification are not eligible for engagement as apprentices.

faq.question: Q8. Will completing the training guarantee a job at DRDO or DESIDOC?
faq.answer: No. Completion of apprenticeship training does not confer any right to employment. Apprentices cannot claim any appointment in DESIDOC or DRDO after the training period ends.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process?
faq.answer: Candidates are shortlisted on merit (marks percentage in the essential qualification) and then called for a written test and/or interview. Only selected candidates are informed through an offer letter or email.
